objective: Create a durable session error patterns KB, validate its required schema and seed signatures, and index it in the self-learning loop so future sessions retrieve exact correction paths.
scope_boundary: Add a dedicated session-error runbook for operator/agent lookup.
Index the runbook in existing self-learning lookup and report output.
Add deterministic validation for required runbook fields and required seed signatures.
Keep the external services runbook scoped to services rather than session tooling errors.
out_of_scope: Changing external service authentication or runtime procedures.
Implementing the remaining epic child issues #534, #536, or #537 in this branch.
research_summary: Existing self-learning already loads fail-fast rows, canonical error patterns, closeouts, and operator context. The gap is a compact session-error source with exact signatures and deterministic schema validation. A new runbook is preferable to expanding the external services runbook because these failures are session/tooling and governance-flow issues, not service operations.

sprints.name: Session error KB and self-learning indexing
sprints.goal: Make recurring session errors searchable and validated without adding a parallel KB layer.
tasks: Create the session-error runbook with the required schema and seed entries.
Add a self-learning loader for the runbook and include session entries in report output.
Add a runbook schema validator and wire it into existing error schema guards.
Record fail-fast, canonical pattern, feature registry, progress, validation, and closeout evidence.
acceptance_criteria: The runbook contains exact signature, category, root cause, correction, guardrail, validation, related files, first observed, and prevented by fields.

Required seed signatures for hook false positives, Claude stream-json/verbose, force-push policy, stale SHA, malformed local_overrides, typoed governance root, PR pending/merge flow, and SQL/schema drift are present.
Self-learning lookup can return runbook entries with source paths and evidence paths.
Self-learning report JSON and markdown list session-error patterns.
Existing error schema hooks and reusable workflow validate the runbook when present.
Stage 6 closeout, handoff, execution scope, and validation artifacts are present before merge.

specialist_reviews.focus: Runbook scope, self-learning indexing, schema validation, and Stage 6 evidence requirements
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended a new session-error runbook instead of bloating the external services runbook, self-learning source indexing, report-level discoverability, and a deterministic schema/seed guard.
evidence: Subagent Popper review in the issue #535 session

alternate_model_review.summary: Specialist review confirmed the implementation must add a dedicated runbook, self-learning loader/report evidence, schema validation, and Stage 6 closeout proof.
evidence: Subagent Popper review in the issue #535 session

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation may update only session-error KB docs, self-learning indexing/reporting, runbook validation, existing error schema guard wiring, and issue #535 evidence artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement runbook indexing, schema validation, report proof, and lifecycle evidence.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Self-learning report output lists session_error_pattern entries.
Implementation stays inside allowed write paths.
Stage 6 closeout evidence is present before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If runbook validation needs a schema migration shared with downstream repos, split it into a follow-up issue.
If an entry requires service operational procedure detail, keep the procedure in the service runbook and link only the session signature here.
If self-learning report output becomes too verbose, retain JSON detail and cap markdown rather than removing source discoverability.
